# UC-22.4.15 · CCPA/CPRA — Automated Decision Profiling — Feature Drift Alerts on Consumer-Profile Models

## Description

Profiling systems should not silently change behavior. Tracking population-level distribution shifts in model inputs (e.g., spend deciles) supports governance that consumers receive consistent, explainable outcomes relative to disclosures.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ml and sourcetypes profile:feature_snapshot. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Calibrate `delta_pct` with model risk team; (2) exclude planned campaign seasonality via lookup; (3) trigger model card update workflow; (4) document consumer-facing impact assessment; (5) pair with fairness testing outside Splunk. Indexes required: `index=ml`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=profile:feature_snapshot`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Model observability HEC (`whylogs`, Evidently, or custom).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ml` `sourcetype="profile:feature_snapshot"` (model_name, feature_name, cohort, mean_val, p95_val, snapshot_date).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Calibrate `delta_pct` with model risk team; (2) exclude planned campaign seasonality via lookup; (3) trigger model card update workflow; (4) document consumer-facing impact assessment; (5) pair with fairness testing outside Splunk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ml sourcetype="profile:feature_snapshot" model_name="marketing_propensity" earliest=-120d
| eval sd=strptime(snapshot_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| sort feature_name, sd
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(mean_val) as prev_mean by feature_name
| eval delta_pct=if(isnotnull(prev_mean) AND prev_mean!=0, abs(mean_val-prev_mean)/abs(prev_mean)*100, null())
| where delta_pct>25
| table feature_name, sd, prev_mean, mean_val, delta_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA — Automated Decision Profiling — Feature Drift Alerts on Consumer-Profile Models** — Profiling systems should not silently change behavior. Tracking population-level distribution shifts in model inputs (e.g., spend deciles) supports governance that consumers receive consistent, explainable outcomes relative to disclosures.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ml` `sourcetype="profile:feature_snapshot"` (model_name, feature_name, cohort, mean_val, p95_val, snapshot_date). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Model observability HEC (`whylogs`, Evidently, or custom). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ml; **sourcetype**: profile:feature_snapshot. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ml, sourcetype="profile:feature_snapshot", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sd** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by feature_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta_pct** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where delta_pct>25` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: delta_pct, feature_name, mean_val, prev_mean, sd.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (mean_val over time), Table (drift events), Single value (features drifted).

## SPL

```spl
index=ml sourcetype="profile:feature_snapshot" model_name="marketing_propensity" earliest=-120d
| eval sd=strptime(snapshot_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| sort feature_name, sd
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(mean_val) as prev_mean by feature_name
| eval delta_pct=if(isnotnull(prev_mean) AND prev_mean!=0, abs(mean_val-prev_mean)/abs(prev_mean)*100, null())
| where delta_pct>25
| table feature_name, sd, prev_mean, mean_val, delta_pct
```

## Visualization

Line chart (mean_val over time), Table (drift events), Single value (features drifted).
